questions.question: NABARD has sanctioned financial assistance of Rs 4,013 crore (the highest in the country) to which state?
questions.explanation: The National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development has sanctioned financial assistance of Rs 4,013 crore to Odisha, the highest in the country. The assistance has been sanctioned for critical rural infrastructure development under the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) during 2021-22.

questions.question: Biplobi Bharat Gallery was recently inaugurated at which city?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the Biplobi Bharat Gallery at Victoria Memorial Hall, Kolkata via video conferencing on the occasion of Shaheed Diwas. The Gallery showcases the contribution of the Revolutionaries in the freedom struggle and their resistance to British colonial rule. It showcases the birth of the revolutionary movement, formation of associations by revolutionary leaders, spread of the movement, formation of Indian National Army, contribution of Naval Mutiny, among others.

questions.question: What is the name of the new Entrance Test conducted for all Central universities?
questions.explanation: The new Common University Education Test (CUET), to be conducted by the National Testing Agency (NTA), is compulsory for all the Central Universities. The exam will be a computer-based, multiple-choice test. The University Grants Commission (UGC) also asks all state, deemed-to-be, and private universities to use the test scores for admissions to undergraduate programmes across the country.

questions.question: Dmitry Muratov, who was seen in the news recently, is from which country?
questions.explanation: The joint winner of last year’s Nobel Peace Prize from Russia, Dmitry Muratov, announced that he will donate his medal to help Ukrainian refugees. Dmitry Muratov, editor of Russia’s leading opposition newspaper Novaya Gazeta, was awarded the 2021 prize, along with Maria Ressa of the Philippines for their efforts ‘to safeguard freedom of expression’.

questions.question: Solomon Islands, which has drafted a security deal with China, is located in which Ocean/Sea?
questions.explanation: Solomon Islands, a country of hundreds of islands in the South-Pacific, has confirmed that it is drafting a security deal with China. As per media reports, a Chinese military base could potentially be set up on the island to Australia’s north. This step has triggered concern in neighbouring Australia and other Western allies in the Indo-Pacific.

questions.question: Which Indian state launched the ‘Udaan project’ to ensure access of schooling and scholarships to rural girls?
questions.explanation: The Udaan project was launched by the Rajasthan government in collaboration with a consulting group ‘IPE Global’. The project aims to keep girls in schools through their enrolment at the secondary level and also ensures that the eligible ones get their scholarships. It has strengthened the scholarship delivery system through IT-enabled services.

questions.question: As per recent official data, what is the average rise in sea surface temperature of Indian Ocean per decade?
questions.explanation: Union Ministry of Earth Sciences recently announced that the tropical Indian Ocean has experienced a rapid increase in ocean warming. The average rise in sea surface temperature of about 1 degree Celsius (°C) was observed over the period of 1951-2015, at 0.15°C per decade. The western Indian Ocean region experienced a four-fold rise in marine heat-waves events and the North Bay of Bengal experienced a two-to-three fold rise.

questions.question: TEJAS Skilling Project to train overseas Indians, was launched in which country?
questions.explanation: Union Minister Anurag Thakur launched TEJAS (Training for Emirates Jobs And Skills) in Dubai, UAE. It is a Skill India International Project to train overseas Indians. The programme aims to skilling, certification and overseas employment of Indians. During the initial phase, it aims at creating a 10,000 strong Indian workforce in UAE. He also invited UAE for collaboration in Animation, Visual Effects, Gaming and Comics (AVGC) Sector.

questions.question: ‘Varnika’ Ink Manufacturing Unit of Bharatiya Reserve Bank Note Mudran Private Limited (BRBNMPL) has been inaugurated in which city?
questions.explanation: Governor of Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Shaktikanta Das dedicated Varnika, the Ink Manufacturing Unit of Bharatiya Reserve Bank Note Mudran Private Limited (BRBNMPL) to the nation in Mysuru. It is a wholly owned subsidiary of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). BRBNMPL has set up Varnika with an annual ink manufacturing capacity of 1,500 MT to enhance the security of banknotes. It aims to achieve cost efficiency and reduced import dependency.

questions.question: The Chartered Accountants Bill seeks to set up a coordination committee headed by?
questions.explanation: The Chartered Accountants, Cost and Works Accountants and Company Secretaries (Amendment) Bill seeks to appoint non-chartered accountant (CA), non-cost accountant and non-company secretary as the presiding officer of the disciplinary committees of the respective bodies. The Bill seeks to set up a coordination committee headed by the corporate affairs secretary. It will have representations from the three institutes: Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI), Institute of Cost Accountants of India (formerly known as ICWAI), and Institute of Company Secretaries of India (ICSI).
